name: Old-Fashioned Deep Chocolate Layer Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, cut into 4 pieces
1/2 cup plus 3 tablespoons unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Butter three 9-inch diameter cake pans with 1/2-inch-high sides.
3. Line pan bottoms with parchment or waxed paper and dust pans with flour, tapping out excess.
4. For the cake: Sift flour, cocoa, baking soda, and salt into a work bowl. Process for about 5 seconds.
5. Add eggs, then sugar to the work bowl. Process until thick, about 1 minute.
6. Add butter and vanilla; process until fluffy, stopping to scrape down sides of the work bowl, about 1 minute.
7. Mix dry ingredients with egg mixture alternately with buttermilk in about 3 additions each, pulsing just to combine each addition.
8. Divide batter evenly among prepared pans (about 1/2 cup in each).
9. Bake until a tester inserted in the center comes out clean, about 24 minutes. Cool cakes on racks for 10 minutes.
10. Run a sharp knife around pan sides to loosen cakes; turn out onto the rack and peel off paper. Cool.
11. For the frosting: Melt chocolate and butter in a heavy small saucepan over low heat, stirring until smooth (or microwave: Place chocolate and butter in a 4-cup microwave-safe bowl; cook uncovered on High for 1 minute, stir well, then cook on High until almost melted, about 30 seconds longer, stirring until smooth).
12. Sift powdered sugar, cocoa, and salt into the work bowl; process for about 5 seconds.
13. Add chocolate mixture and vanilla; process until just combined, about 5 seconds.
14. Add 4 tablespoons sour cream; process to blend, about 8 seconds.
15. Scrape down sides of the work bowl and thin to spreadable consistency by adding enough remaining sour cream by tablespoons, processing after each addition.
16. Place one cake layer on a serving platter and spread with 1/2 cup frosting. Top with second cake layer and spread with 1/2 cup frosting. Top with third cake layer and spread the remaining frosting over the top and sides of the cake.
17. Serve immediately or prepare up to 12 hours in advance, refrigerating until frosting is firm, then cover and keep refrigerated. Bring to room temperature before serving.
12 servings
